title: themeda triandra var. glauca
intro: Themeda triandra var. glauca, also known as the blue grass or glaucous Themeda, is a variety of Themeda triandra, a type of grass native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and bronchitis. Themeda triandra var. glauca can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
